title: /bin/sh: line 1: systemctl: command not found
body:
### Issue Description

$ podman image build -f plexmediaserver.imgbuild -t plex .
... fails with the above.  podman supports systemd but does not have systemctl.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. $ podman image build -f plexmediaserver.imgbuild -t plex .
STEP 1/9: FROM registry.opensuse.org/opensuse/tumbleweed
STEP 2/9: RUN echo && echo "**** Install extras ****"   && zypper in -y                 lsof            mawk            nano   && ln -s /usr/bin/mawk /usr/bin/awk
--> Using cache f1f5c5d9220f114fe5982d10bcb2d344caaf388fae877de8c8e680d0cd534e84
--> f1f5c5d9220f
STEP 3/9: COPY /plex.repo /etc/zypp/repos.d/
--> Using cache 513b559cc9f2e4f34c63fe36fdc215699b99e184cfc5a0c666898a0067db5de8
--> 513b559cc9f2
STEP 4/9: RUN   echo "systemd" > /proc/1/comm   && echo && echo "**** Install Plex repo ****"   && zypper --gpg-auto-import-keys ref    && zypper in -y                 plexmediaserver         && sed -i 's/User=plex//g' /usr/lib/systemd/system/plexmediaserver.service      && sed -i 's/Group=plex//g' /usr/lib/systemd/system/plexmediaserver.service
--> Using cache b1ced7e6e36e81ced3c6a57f14a2d72b7438377b556f166146771822ce046185
--> b1ced7e6e36e
STEP 5/9: RUN   systemctl daemon-reload
/bin/sh: line 1: systemctl: command not found
Error: building at STEP "RUN systemctl daemon-reload": while running runtime: exit status 127

2. $ cat plex.imgbuild
FROM registry.opensuse.org/opensuse/tumbleweed

RUN echo && echo "**** Install extras ****" \
        && zypper in -y \
                lsof \
                mawk \
                nano \
        && ln -s /usr/bin/mawk /usr/bin/awk

COPY /plex.repo /etc/zypp/repos.d/

        # Fake out the PMS install script to make it think systemd:
RUN     echo "systemd" > /proc/1/comm \

        && echo && echo "**** Install Plex repo ****" \

        && zypper --gpg-auto-import-keys ref \
        && zypper in -y \
                plexmediaserver \

        # Remove 'plex' user:group so Plex runs as root (bill) to get all dirs
        && sed -i 's/User=plex//g' /usr/lib/systemd/system/plexmediaserver.service \
        && sed -i 's/Group=plex//g' /usr/lib/systemd/system/plexmediaserver.service


RUN     systemctl daemon-reload
RUN systemctl enable plexmediaserver.service

RUN   echo && echo "**** Cleanup ****" \
  && rm -rf \
        $HOME/.cache \
        /tmp/* \
        /var/tmp/* \

  && echo && echo "**** Expose Port ****"
# 32400/tcp PM Server, 1900/udp/32469/tcp Plex DLNA Server, 32410/32412/32413/32414/udp GDM network discovery
EXPOSE 32400/tcp

CMD [ "/usr/lib/systemd/systemd" ]



### Describe the results you received

build crashes with "/bin/sh: line 1: systemctl: command not found"

I've asked on IRC but nobody's there.

### Describe the results you expected

Expected the image to build properly.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: It's not finding the binary of `systemctl` to run.  Near as I can tell, the base image doesn't include that command, because it doesn't have systemd installed in it.

Understand, but podman is supposed to have systemd support, meaning it adds the binaries on-the-fly.  As I can not get an image built nor a container started, I can't check for this.

podman should automatically add systemctl since it's advertised to have systemd support and since I call for it in CMD..

That is not what supporting systemd means.  Running systemd in a container requires that the process of starting a container at run time be done a bit differently from how it would otherwise be done.
The build process for images does not add content to them as a side-effect of a CMD instruction.

So you're saying that built-in support of systemd is no good if you need to start a service within the container with systemd.

I don't understand what then was the rationale for adding -any- systemd...  if in fact it was added?

Certain things need to be done differently if the main process for the container is an init system, such as systemd, which will itself be starting and managing multiple services within that container.

When booting in a VM or on bare metal, systemd expects to be mounting filesystems and doing mundane things like seeding the system random number generator, and the correct thing to do when any of that fails (as it would in a container which doesn't allow any of that) is to either fail completely or drop to a recovery shell.  When it's running in a container, it depends on the engine that started the container to take care of certain things on its behalf, and it, in turn, needs to be given some indication that it can and should skip these things.  That is what part of what it means for podman to support running systemd in a container.

Getting the systemd binaries into a container image while it's being built, in particular binaries that are built for the right architecture and linked against the shared libraries that are also going to be in that image, along with their dependencies, is something that has to be done using ADD, COPY, or RUN.

Ok so systemd support in podman does not actually include the binaries necessary to start services within the container.

So assuming that adding the systemd package to the image, tis will -not- interfere with whatever mechanisms of systemd are built into podman.   So my new imgbuild is as so:
FROM registry.opensuse.org/opensuse/tumbleweed

RUN echo && echo "**** Install extras ****" \
        && zypper in -y \
#               iptables-backend-nft \
                lsof \
                mawk \
                nano \
                systemd \
        && ln -s /usr/bin/mawk /usr/bin/awk

COPY /plex.repo /etc/zypp/repos.d/

        # Fake out the PMS install script to make it think systemd:
RUN     echo "systemd" > /proc/1/comm \

        && echo && echo "**** Install Plex repo ****" \

        && zypper --gpg-auto-import-keys ref \
        && zypper in -y \
                plexmediaserver \

        # Remove 'plex' user:group so Plex runs as root (bill) to get all dirs
        && sed -i 's/User=plex//g' /usr/lib/systemd/system/plexmediaserver.service \
        && sed -i 's/Group=plex//g' /usr/lib/systemd/system/plexmediaserver.service


RUN     systemctl daemon-reload
RUN systemctl enable plexmediaserver.service

RUN   echo && echo "**** Cleanup ****" \
  && rm -rf \
        $HOME/.cache \
        /tmp/* \
        /var/tmp/* \

  && echo && echo "**** Expose Port ****"
# 32400/tcp PM Server, 1900/udp/32469/tcp Plex DLNA Server, 32410/32412/32413/32414/udp GDM network discovery
EXPOSE 32400/tcp

#RUN echo && echo "**** Set volume ****"
#VOLUME /var/lib/plexmediaserver/
# /srv/cygnus/ /srv/vela/complete/

#RUN echo "" && echo "**** force library dependencies ****" && \
#export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/lib #required to use /lib/ld-musl.x86_64.so instead of Plex's version && \
#export LD_PRELOAD=/usr/lib/libva.so.2:/usr/lib/plexmediaserver/lib/libssl.so.3:/usr/lib/plexmediaserver/lib/libcrypto.so.3 #us>

CMD [ "/usr/lib/systemd/systemd" ]
======================================================
$ podman image build -f plexmediaserver.imgbuild -t plex .
STEP 1/9: FROM registry.opensuse.org/opensuse/tumbleweed
STEP 2/9: RUN echo && echo "**** Install extras ****"   && zypper in -y                 lsof            mawk            nano   systemd  && ln -s /usr/bin/mawk /usr/bin/awk
--> Using cache 7f419035e458bd3a22ed7b0b5a246247be3857c6766b8a616e9a76925e0e41e9
--> 7f419035e458
STEP 3/9: COPY /plex.repo /etc/zypp/repos.d/
--> Using cache af9bac09d2e81391f01d52a7a399b45f75dabc7f9098f009a0fe4390442f32ef
--> af9bac09d2e8
STEP 4/9: RUN   echo "systemd" > /proc/1/comm   && echo && echo "**** Install Plex repo ****"   && zypper --gpg-auto-import-keys ref    && zypper in -y                 plexmediaserver         && sed -i 's/User=plex//g' /usr/lib/systemd/system/plexmediaserver.service      && sed -i 's/Group=plex//g' /usr/lib/systemd/system/plexmediaserver.service
--> Using cache af0f965a44406894ffc8423171e7687d5f2afe9e2367ac65e19b7f94a0de82e4
--> af0f965a4440
STEP 5/9: RUN   systemctl daemon-reload
error running container: from /usr/bin/crun creating container for [/bin/sh -c systemctl daemon-reload]: sd-bus call: Process org.freedesktop.systemd1 exited with status 1: Input/output error
: exit status 1
ERRO[0000] did not get container create message from subprocess: EOF 
Error: building at STEP "RUN systemctl daemon-reload": while running runtime: exit status 1
Oh what a mess this forum makes of code...

Changing the recorded command which was used to launch the shell for a RUN instruction is not a substitute for having an init system.  But then if there isn't an init system running (and running processes don't persist across instructions during a build), it doesn't need to be told to reload its configuration when the files which contain that configuration are modified.

First you need to install into the container systemd and its tools, podman does not automatically add them to containers, it only executes code installed into the container.  The `podman run` command when seeing a CMD of systemd, will configure the container environment in a way that systemd likes.

You are doing  a podman build not a podman run. In the Build steps each command is a separate container only running that command.  Systemd would not be running inside of the RUN container, so `systemctl daemon-reload` will fail. However this line is not even needed, since when systemd starts it automatically does a daemon reload.
Maybe these are things that should be mentioned in the docs.  It may be self-evident to devs, but is a knowledge-gap and show-stopper for others.
would you care to create a PR?

Github is one of those things I've sworn I will never learn...  like fixing automatic transmissions.

I am preparing to start a wiki though with all the painful struggles I've had and lessons learned in the Black Arts of cantankerous podman.
Well you are welcome to try your luck with other container technology like Docker, but you are going to find the same issues.  Since this is not a bug I am going to close it.

I see.  So this is your attitude.

Well in that case it's not worth it to set up a wiki on podman explaining all the gaps in knowledge and filling them in.  I have better things to do.
